name: New York, New York
seo_title: New York, New York Sales Tax Calculator
meta_description: Find the current sales tax in New York, New York. Enter a price and category in the calculator to see the exact tax and total, broken down by state, county, and local district.
intro_text: Use the calculator below to find the current sales tax in New York, New York. Enter a purchase amount to see the exact tax, broken down by state, county, and local district.
seo_content:
How sales tax works in New York, New York

Sales tax in New York isn't a single flat rate — it's New York's statewide rate combined with local taxes added by New York County and any city or special districts that cover the address. Because those local pieces differ from one area to the next, the total can vary between ZIP codes even within the same city. The New York sales tax calculator above looks up the current rate for the address, so you always see today's figure rather than one that's drifted out of date.

Calculating sales tax on a purchase

Enter the price in the calculator above and choose a category. It applies New York's current combined rate and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to New York County, and to local districts. Because the rate updates automatically, the total stays accurate even after local rates change.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how New York treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why does the rate differ between nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and come out slightly higher or lower, even when the state portion is identical.

How often does the rate change? Statewide rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes — usually at the start of a calendar quarter. That's why the calculator looks up the current rate instead of relying on a fixed number, and why Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction for the businesses that file here.

meta_description: Find the current sales tax in Charlotte, North Carolina. Enter a price and category in the calculator to see the exact tax and total, broken down by state, county, and local district.
intro_text: Use the calculator below to find the current sales tax in Charlotte, North Carolina. Enter a purchase amount to see the exact tax, broken down by state, county, and local district.
seo_content:
How sales tax works in Charlotte, North Carolina

Sales tax in Charlotte isn't a single flat rate — it's North Carolina's statewide rate combined with local taxes added by Mecklenburg County and any city or special districts that cover the address. Because those local pieces differ from one area to the next, the total can vary between ZIP codes even within the same city. The Charlotte sales tax calculator above looks up the current rate for the address, so you always see today's figure rather than one that's drifted out of date.

Calculating sales tax on a purchase

Enter the price in the calculator above and choose a category. It applies Charlotte's current combined rate and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Mecklenburg County, and to local districts. Because the rate updates automatically, the total stays accurate even after local rates change.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how North Carolina treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why does the rate differ between nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and come out slightly higher or lower, even when the state portion is identical.

How often does the rate change? Statewide rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes — usually at the start of a calendar quarter. That's why the calculator looks up the current rate instead of relying on a fixed number, and why Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction for the businesses that file here.

name: Portland, Oregon
meta_description: Portland, Oregon has no statewide sales tax. Use the calculator to confirm how product categories are treated and see your total.
intro_text: Portland, Oregon doesn't charge a statewide sales tax. Use the calculator below to confirm how specific product categories are treated.
seo_content:
Does Portland, Oregon have sales tax?

No. Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Portland generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Portland, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Portland

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Portland don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.

seo_content:
Does Salem, Oregon have sales tax?

No. Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Salem generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Salem, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Salem

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Salem don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.

Does Eugene, Oregon have sales tax?

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Eugene, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Eugene don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Does Hillsboro, Oregon have sales tax?

No. Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Hillsboro generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Hillsboro, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Hillsboro don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Does Beaverton, Oregon have sales tax?

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Beaverton, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

No. New Hampshire is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Nashua generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Nashua, which is one reason New Hampshire draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Nashua

Because New Hampshire has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Nashua don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. seo_content:
Does Bend, Oregon have sales tax?

No. Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Bend generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Bend, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Bend

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Bend don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.

intro_text: Use the calculator below to find the current sales tax in Fayetteville, Arkansas. Enter a purchase amount to see the exact tax, broken down by state, county, and local district.
seo_content:
How sales tax works in Fayetteville, Arkansas

Sales tax in Fayetteville isn't a single flat rate — it's Arkansas's statewide rate combined with local taxes added by Washington County and any city or special districts that cover the address. Because those local pieces differ from one area to the next, the total can vary between ZIP codes even within the same city. The Fayetteville sales tax calculator above looks up the current rate for the address, so you always see today's figure rather than one that's drifted out of date.

Calculating sales tax on a purchase

Enter the price in the calculator above and choose a category. It applies Fayetteville's current combined rate and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Washington County, and to local districts. Because the rate updates automatically, the total stays accurate even after local rates change.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how Arkansas treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why does the rate differ between nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and come out slightly higher or lower, even when the state portion is identical.

How often does the rate change? Statewide rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes — usually at the start of a calendar quarter. That's why the calculator looks up the current rate instead of relying on a fixed number, and why Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction for the businesses that file here.

name: Medford, Oregon
meta_description: Medford, Oregon has no statewide sales tax. Use the calculator to confirm how product categories are treated and see your total.
intro_text: Medford, Oregon doesn't charge a statewide sales tax. Use the calculator below to confirm how specific product categories are treated.
seo_content:
Does Medford, Oregon have sales tax?

No. Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Medford generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Medford, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Medford

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Medford don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.

intro_text: Use the calculator below to find the current sales tax in Folsom, California. Enter a purchase amount to see the exact tax, broken down by state, county, and local district.
seo_content:
How sales tax works in Folsom, California

Sales tax in Folsom isn't a single flat rate — it's California's statewide rate combined with local taxes added by Sacramento County and any city or special districts that cover the address. Because those local pieces differ from one area to the next, the total can vary between ZIP codes even within the same city. The Folsom sales tax calculator above looks up the current rate for the address, so you always see today's figure rather than one that's drifted out of date.

Calculating sales tax on a purchase

Enter the price in the calculator above and choose a category. It applies Folsom's current combined rate and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Sacramento County, and to local districts. Because the rate updates automatically, the total stays accurate even after local rates change.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how California treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why does the rate differ between nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and come out slightly higher or lower, even when the state portion is identical.

How often does the rate change? Statewide rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes — usually at the start of a calendar quarter. That's why the calculator looks up the current rate instead of relying on a fixed number, and why Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction for the businesses that file here.

name: Wilmington, Delaware
meta_description: Wilmington, Delaware has no statewide sales tax. Use the calculator to confirm how product categories are treated and see your total.
intro_text: Wilmington, Delaware doesn't charge a statewide sales tax. Use the calculator below to confirm how specific product categories are treated.
seo_content:
Does Wilmington, Delaware have sales tax?

No. Delaware is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Wilmington generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Wilmington, which is one reason Delaware draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Wilmington

Because Delaware has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Wilmington don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.

intro_text: Use the calculator below to find the current sales tax in Palatine, Illinois. Enter a purchase amount to see the exact tax, broken down by state, county, and local district.
seo_content:
How sales tax works in Palatine, Illinois

Sales tax in Palatine isn't a single flat rate — it's Illinois's statewide rate combined with local taxes added by Cook County and any city or special districts that cover the address. Because those local pieces differ from one area to the next, the total can vary between ZIP codes even within the same city. The Palatine sales tax calculator above looks up the current rate for the address, so you always see today's figure rather than one that's drifted out of date.

Calculating sales tax on a purchase

Enter the price in the calculator above and choose a category. It applies Palatine's current combined rate and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Cook County, and to local districts. Because the rate updates automatically, the total stays accurate even after local rates change.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how Illinois treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why does the rate differ between nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and come out slightly higher or lower, even when the state portion is identical.

How often does the rate change? Statewide rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes — usually at the start of a calendar quarter. That's why the calculator looks up the current rate instead of relying on a fixed number, and why Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction for the businesses that file here.

name: Missoula, Montana
meta_description: Missoula, Montana has no statewide sales tax. Use the calculator to confirm how product categories are treated and see your total.
intro_text: Missoula, Montana doesn't charge a statewide sales tax. Use the calculator below to confirm how specific product categories are treated.
seo_content:
Does Missoula, Montana have sales tax?

No. Montana is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Missoula generally aren't taxed at the register — the price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. The calculator above confirms this and lets you check how individual product categories are treated.

What this means for shoppers

You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts in Missoula, which is one reason Montana draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Missoula

Because Montana has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Missoula don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, though, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.
